title: Function calling in chat
summary: Function calling inside a stateful chat session — tools are declared once on the chat, automatic execution carries across every turn, and call history accumulates with the conversation.
notes: Tools are declared on chats.create — every send_message inherits them, so you do not re-pass the tools list per turn.
Each turn's automatic_function_calling_history is *cumulative across the chat* — index from the previous turn's length to count calls per turn rather than per session.

Tool-call tokens land in tool_use_prompt_token_count; for a long chat with many short tool calls this can dominate input cost. Compare against text.tool-call (single-shot) on the same prompt to see the per-turn overhead.

Flash-Lite tiers (gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview, gemini-2.5-flash-lite) are intentionally omitted: function calling inside a stateful chats.create session is unreliable on Lite in practice, despite the model card listing function calling as supported. Use 3 Flash or higher for chat-with-tools; Flash-Lite is fine for single-shot tool calls — see text.tool-call.
